skill.name: trade-show-finder
skill.description: Find and compare trade shows, exhibitions, expos, and industry events by industry, region, date range, audience, and business goal.
skill.summary: Research trade shows and compare event options for attendance or exhibiting decisions.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high risk, but review found the high-severity crypto, obfuscation, path traversal, and shell execution alerts are false positives from markdown prose, code fences, Chinese text, and relative documentation links. The only confirmed concern is low-risk network exposure because the skill instructs the assistant to use web search and includes hardcoded external URLs, including a recurring Lensmor promotional link.

low_findings.title: External Web Research and Promotional Links
low_findings.description: The skill asks the assistant to use web search for current event information and includes hardcoded external URLs in examples and output guidance. This is expected for trade show research, but the Lensmor links with UTM parameters create a low-risk outbound traffic and promotion concern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain visible external URLs and explicit web research instructions. I found no evidence that secrets or local data are collected or sent to those URLs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts correspond to markdown fenced examples and output templates, not executable Ruby code or runtime shell execution. No scripts or command execution implementation were found in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown code fences showing prompts, shell install text, or desired output format. There is no executable source file or dynamic command construction in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts are relative markdown links to sibling skill documentation. They do not instruct the assistant to read arbitrary filesystem paths or access parent directories for data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to documentation links such as related skill references. No filesystem operation or user-controlled path handling exists in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Pattern in Natural Language
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match substrings in normal markdown text, not calls to DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any cryptographic library. No cryptographic operation is implemented by this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are descriptive prose and markdown output guidance. I found no code that imports or invokes cryptographic algorithms.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Chinese README Entropy
low_findings.description: The entropy alert is caused by Chinese-language documentation, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content. The file is readable markdown that mirrors the English README at a shorter length.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed normal localized markdown content. No encoded payload, binary blob, or prompt injection text was present in the file.

content.user_title: Find Trade Shows by Industry and Region
content.value_statement: Trade show research is time consuming because dates, locations, and audience data are spread across many sources. This skill structures the search, compares events, and recommends shows that match a business goal.
actual_capabilities: Collects trade show candidates by industry, region, date range, and goal.
Builds comparison tables with dates, venues, audience size, and focus areas.
Ranks events by relevance instead of alphabetical order.
Adds recommendations for attending, exhibiting, or competitor scouting.
Flags uncertain dates, estimated statistics, cancellations, or postponements when known.
Suggests next steps such as registration timing, travel considerations, and adjacent events.
limitations: Requires current web research for dates, venues, and event statistics.
May rely on estimates when official exhibitor or visitor counts are unavailable.
Does not book booths, register attendees, or contact event organizers.
Recommendations depend on the detail provided about industry, region, budget, and goals.

use_cases.title: Plan an Annual Event Calendar
use_cases.description: Identify priority trade shows for the next year and compare which events fit campaign goals and regional targets.

use_cases.title: Choose Where to Exhibit
use_cases.description: Compare audience size, buyer profile, and show focus before committing booth budget to one or more events.

use_cases.title: Research a New Market
use_cases.description: Find relevant exhibitions in a target country or sector to understand competitors, distributors, and buyer demand.

prompt_templates.title: Find Events for an Industry
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search with clear industry and region.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find trade shows for the packaging industry in Europe during 2026. Include dates, locations, and top recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Named Shows
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare specific events before choosing one.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare CES, MWC, and IFA for a consumer electronics startup. Recommend the best show for meeting distributors.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Market Entry Calendar
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan multiple events around a business objective.
prompt_templates.prompt: We sell surgical instruments and want to enter the EU market. Build a 2026 trade show shortlist with best-fit reasons and timing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize Exhibiting Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced selection with constraints and decision criteria.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find industrial automation trade shows in Asia for the next 18 months. Prioritize events for exhibitor ROI, distributor access, and competitor scouting.

output_examples.input: Find medical device trade shows in Europe for 2026.
output_examples.output: A ranked table of European medical device events with dates, venues, exhibitor counts, visitor counts, focus areas, and three top recommendations.

output_examples.input: Should we attend CES, MWC, or IFA as a consumer electronics startup?
output_examples.output: A side-by-side comparison with audience fit, startup relevance, buyer profile, cost considerations, and a clear recommendation.

output_examples.input: What trade shows help us meet distributors in the Middle East?
output_examples.output: A shortlist of regional events with distributor relevance, expected audience, travel context, and practical next steps for outreach.

best_practices: Provide industry, region, date range, and whether the goal is attending, exhibiting, or scouting competitors.
Ask for official sources and recent edition statistics when decisions depend on exact attendance or exhibitor counts.
Use the recommendation section to compare business fit, not only event size.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague requests such as “find events” without an industry or target region.
Do not treat estimated exhibitor or visitor counts as guaranteed current figures.
Do not rely on the skill to replace official registration, visa, booth, or travel confirmation.

faq.question: Does this skill need web access?
faq.answer: Yes. Current dates, venues, and statistics should be verified with web search and official event sources.

faq.question: Can it compare specific trade shows?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the show names and your business goal, and it can compare fit, audience, timing, and tradeoffs.

faq.question: Can it recommend shows for exhibiting?
faq.answer: Yes. It can prioritize events by audience match, market reach, booth value, and competitor presence when data is available.

faq.question: Does it cover international exhibitions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can research events by country, region, city, or global market area.

faq.question: How accurate are visitor and exhibitor counts?
faq.answer: They depend on available sources. The skill should mark uncertain figures as estimates or to be confirmed.

faq.question: Can it register me for an event?
faq.answer: No. It provides research and recommendations, but registration and booth booking must be completed through official channels.
